use_when: reach for FanOut when one input must be broadcast to N independent sink pipes (mirroring, shadow/canary traffic, audit logging) at a cost of N-1 clones instead of hand-rolling a loop over sinks
reach for FanIn when N pollable sources must merge into one stream, round-robin fair, without a perpetually-ready source starving the rest, instead of hand-rolling a poll loop over the sources
reach for Filter when an inbound item should be admitted or short-circuited by a pure Decide<In> predicate before the inner pipe ever runs, instead of hand-rolling an if/else guard inside every pipe

reach for the DemandGate/AtomicGate/Demand vocabulary when a pipe needs SHED (reject now), WAIT (go dormant, no-op call), or BALANCE (skip an unready backend in a FanIn) readiness semantics, instead of adding a bespoke poll_ready trait method

reach for BoundedQueue plus its FailMode/Demand/BatchSource/Batch/Live family when a producer and a slower consumer disagree about speed and you must pick an explicit loss profile (block, drop-oldest, drop-newest, sample, credit, demand, batch, coalesce), instead of an unbounded channel that OOMs under load

reach for BoundedQueue::enqueue under FailMode::DropOldest when a producer must never block or retry on a full queue and availability matters more than completeness, instead of a lossy queue with hand-rolled overflow handling
reach for RateLimit when calls must be admitted against a per-key token bucket refilled by an injected Clock, instead of hand-rolling a counter-and-timestamp throttle

reach for RetryController::on_outcome (or the production Retry<Inner> wrapper in proxima-primitives/src/pipe/retry.rs) when a failed attempt should decide Retry/Done/Exhausted from a Retryable outcome plus RetryRules, instead of hand-rolling a retry loop with ad hoc status checks
reach for the Pipe trait (typed In -> Out via an async call) as the base unit for any source, transform, sink, or observe role, instead of inventing a bespoke handler signature per role
reach for Fallback<P, S> when a primary pipe's failure should route to an alternate pipe sharing In/Out/Err, instead of hand-rolling a try/catch-and-reroute branch in the caller

reach for CircuitBreaker when consecutive failures against a dependency should trip a Closed/Open/HalfOpen state machine that short-circuits calls during a cooldown and probes recovery, instead of hand-rolling a failure counter and cooldown timer
implement FrameCodec (parse_frame/encode_frame) when teaching proxima a new wire protocol's frame layer, instead of hand-rolling buffer parsing/encoding outside the pipe algebra
reach for Deadline::expired(now_nanos) when an operation should time out against an injected Clock rather than a real sleep, so the timeout stays deterministic and testable

compose an attempt-budget Signal (plus an optional sink-side dedup ledger) when a sender needs an explicit at-most-once/at-least-once/exactly-once guarantee over an unreliable sink, instead of assuming a channel's default semantics are enough

reach for Fallthrough selection (cache upstream tried first, a miss falls through to origin) plus WriteBack when fronting a slow origin with a cache, instead of hand-rolling a check-then-populate cache branch in the handler

reach for RecordUpstream when live traffic through a pipe must be teed to a durable JSONL/binary cassette for later replay or audit without changing the pipe's own In/Out shape, instead of hand-rolling request/response logging
reach for ReplayUpstream when tests or fakes need to serve previously recorded traffic byte-identical by a method+path(+body) match key, instead of hand-rolling a mock HTTP server

copy plugin-skeleton's register(builder) -> Result<AppBuilder> convention (built on PluginRegistry/PipeFactory) when packaging a new pipe as a reusable plugin crate, instead of hand-wiring a one-off pipe into every consuming app

reach for a Selection strategy (e.g. RoundRobin over healthy backends, the shape load-balance's own LoadBalancerPipe hand-rolls) when a request must be dispatched to one of N backends by policy, instead of hand-rolling a rotation cursor and health check inline

reach for the Runtime trait when the same sans-IO Pipe instance must be served concurrently by two different executors (e.g. prime and tokio) in one process, instead of assuming only one runtime can drive a process
reach for Signal (fire-once, sticky, awaitable via fired()) when a consumer must wait for a one-time completion event, instead of hand-rolling a polling loop or a bespoke oneshot flag

compose Signal::child (propagating) or Signal::guard (drop-to-cancel) when wiring cooperative, deadline, drop-triggered, propagating, or cleanup-guaranteed cancellation, instead of building a bespoke CancellationToken type
reach for Chaos<Inner> when a test needs seeded, reproducible Error/Dropped/Delay fault injection in front of a pipe under test, instead of hand-rolling nondeterministic failure injection
